---
schema: verdi.decisionconflict/v1
covers: e965774765933bba64c0ad6a25fdc90874747930
findings:
  - { id: judged-f-1, kind: judged, text: "derivation-drawer dc-2 adds a decision-conflict case-file chip to the wall, but parent wall-receipts dc-1 defines the v1 badge set as a closed enumeration (VL lint findings, spec-stale, pending-supersession, empty evidence slots, size-smell) that does not include a decision-conflict-report chip. dc-2 cites wall-receipts dc-1 only for its 'no computation is invented' clause, and no supersedes/exempts edge is declared against the badge-set enumeration the chip extends. (confidence 0.70)", target_ref: spec/wall-receipts }
  - { id: judged-f-2, kind: judged, text: "derivation-drawer dc-3 invents new deterministic comparisons for wall display (report covers vs current spec content digest; decisions_scanned vs currently-declared decision ids), which are not among the 'existing computes only' permitted by wall-receipts dc-1 and functionally overlap the parent's existing spec-stale compute. dc-3's framing that this is drawer disclosure rather than a verdict badge sidesteps the literal badge wording, but the computation is still newly invented for the wall with no declared supersedes/exempts edge. (confidence 0.50)", target_ref: spec/wall-receipts }
  - { id: judged-f-3, kind: judged, text: "wall-receipts dc-2 requires a derivation drawer to name the rule id, the pinned inputs with revisions, and the firing records; derivation-drawer dc-2 specifies its case-file drawer content as findings with disposition states plus a sweep provenance block, which does not explicitly guarantee a rule id or firing records for judge-sweep findings — a divergence from the parent drawer contract with no declared edge. (confidence 0.35)", target_ref: spec/wall-receipts }
sweep_provenance: { adr_corpus_digest: sha256:37517e5f3dc66819f61f5a7bb8ace1921282415f10551d2defa5c3eb0985b570, decisions_scanned: [spec/derivation-drawer#dc-1, spec/derivation-drawer#dc-2, spec/derivation-drawer#dc-3, spec/derivation-drawer#dc-4, spec/wall-receipts#dc-1, spec/wall-receipts#dc-2, spec/wall-receipts#dc-3, spec/wall-receipts#dc-4] }

judge_integrity: { stdin_b64: , raw_result: "```json\n{\"findings\":[{\"id\":\"f-1\",\"text\":\"derivation-drawer dc-2 adds a decision-conflict case-file chip to the wall, but parent wall-receipts dc-1 defines the v1 badge set as a closed enumeration (VL lint findings, spec-stale, pending-supersession, empty evidence slots, size-smell) that does not include a decision-conflict-report chip. dc-2 cites wall-receipts dc-1 only for its 'no computation is invented' clause, and no supersedes/exempts edge is declared against the badge-set enumeration the chip extends.\",\"confidence\":0.7,\"target\":\"spec/wall-receipts\"},{\"id\":\"f-2\",\"text\":\"derivation-drawer dc-3 invents new deterministic comparisons for wall display (report covers vs current spec content digest; decisions_scanned vs currently-declared decision ids), which are not among the 'existing computes only' permitted by wall-receipts dc-1 and functionally overlap the parent's existing spec-stale compute. dc-3's framing that this is drawer disclosure rather than a verdict badge sidesteps the literal badge wording, but the computation is still newly invented for the wall with no declared supersedes/exempts edge.\",\"confidence\":0.5,\"target\":\"spec/wall-receipts\"},{\"id\":\"f-3\",\"text\":\"wall-receipts dc-2 requires a derivation drawer to name the rule id, the pinned inputs with revisions, and the firing records; derivation-drawer dc-2 specifies its case-file drawer content as findings with disposition states plus a sweep provenance block, which does not explicitly guarantee a rule id or firing records for judge-sweep findings — a divergence from the parent drawer contract with no declared edge.\",\"confidence\":0.35,\"target\":\"spec/wall-receipts\"}]}\n```" }

---
# Decision-conflict report

## Computed (declared-edge completeness)

(none)

## Judged (undeclared-conflict sweep)

- **judged-f-1** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: derivation-drawer dc-2 adds a decision-conflict case-file chip to the wall, but parent wall-receipts dc-1 defines the v1 badge set as a closed enumeration (VL lint findings, spec-stale, pending-supersession, empty evidence slots, size-smell) that does not include a decision-conflict-report chip. dc-2 cites wall-receipts dc-1 only for its 'no computation is invented' clause, and no supersedes/exempts edge is declared against the badge-set enumeration the chip extends. (confidence 0.70)
- **judged-f-2** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: derivation-drawer dc-3 invents new deterministic comparisons for wall display (report covers vs current spec content digest; decisions_scanned vs currently-declared decision ids), which are not among the 'existing computes only' permitted by wall-receipts dc-1 and functionally overlap the parent's existing spec-stale compute. dc-3's framing that this is drawer disclosure rather than a verdict badge sidesteps the literal badge wording, but the computation is still newly invented for the wall with no declared supersedes/exempts edge. (confidence 0.50)
- **judged-f-3** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: wall-receipts dc-2 requires a derivation drawer to name the rule id, the pinned inputs with revisions, and the firing records; derivation-drawer dc-2 specifies its case-file drawer content as findings with disposition states plus a sweep provenance block, which does not explicitly guarantee a rule id or firing records for judge-sweep findings — a divergence from the parent drawer contract with no declared edge. (confidence 0.35)
